housing_court_case.court_order: Lifetime tenancy at $950/month with mandatory 2-year leases
housing_court_case.landlord_name: Building Owner (Not fully identified in file)
stipulations_agreed: Lifetime residency - cannot reclaim until tenant death or voluntary surrender
stipulations_violated: Landlord provided 1-year general lease instead of 2-year lease
Added Clause 4: 'Nuisance clause' allowing eviction if tenant becomes nuisance to other tenants
Attempted wrongful eviction based on false non-payment claim

inquest_case.status: Default judgment - tenant did not attend
inquest_case.issue: Landlord used this old decision to attempt wrongful eviction

eviction_attempt.claim: Non-payment of rent (FALSE - rent was current)
eviction_attempt.basis: Used old inquest decision from year prior
eviction_attempt.tenant_response: Court case ongoing - tenant filed OSC

property_loss.status: Major issue - $2+ million in belongings lost
timeline: Tenant obtained OSC (Order to Show Cause) from court (should have halted eviction)
Upon return from court, tenant's belongings were removed from apartment
property_loss.potential_violation: Property removed after court-ordered stay - possible violation of tenant rights

claims: Damages for violation of court order (stay)

criminal_case_impact.situation: Separate criminal case - tenant missed court date due to medical emergency
criminal_case_impact.incident: Passed out on train en route to court, hit head, arrived at court bleeding
criminal_case_impact.hospital_documentation: Hospital ID bands worn during subsequent court appearances
criminal_case_impact.outcome: Pressured to accept plea/settlement despite medical hardship
criminal_case_impact.attorney_issue: Assigned counsel did not provide effective assistance; pressured acceptance

health_and_disability_issues.hospitalization_history: Multiple hospitalizations over 5+ years with recent emergencies
court_attendance_barriers: Inability to travel safely due to medical conditions
Limited capacity to participate fully in court proceedings
evidence: Timeline of hospitalizations vs. court dates
health_and_disability_issues.ada_considerations: Possible ADA violation - court may not have properly accommodated disabilities

legal_issues_summary: Stipulation violation (1-year vs. 2-year lease; nuisance clause added)
Loss of property (~$2 million) after court-ordered stay
Possible violation of stay by marshal
Four ongoing civil lawsuits for damages
Missed court dates due to disability/hospitalization (potential grounds for relief)
Default judgment from inquest (possible motion to vacate)

evidence: Medical records showing hospitalization on date of inquest
motion_to_vacate_inquest.likelihood: Strong - documented medical cause for absence

motion_to_vacate_eviction.basis: Violation of court-ordered stay; wrongful eviction
evidence: Copy of OSC and court-ordered stay
Marshal documents showing removal after stay
motion_to_vacate_eviction.likelihood: Strong - clear violation of court order

ineffective_assistance_of_counsel.basis: Assigned counsel failed to present medical evidence; pressured into unfavorable plea
evidence: Hospital records and ID bands worn in court
Notes on pressure to accept plea
Records of counsel's lack of support

poor_person_status.purpose: Waive court fees, improve access to legal aid

organizations_contacted: Legal Aid Society - WILLING TO HELP (requires open case)
legal_aid_status.current_status: Legal Aid Society requires active/open court case
legal_aid_status.next_step: File motion to reopen case; notify Legal Aid immediately after filing

resources.name: Legal Aid Society
resources.note: Will help once case is active

step_1.title: Gather All Documents
step_1.details: Collect medical records, court notices, eviction papers, marshal documents, storage receipts, landlord communications

step_2.title: Create Timeline and Case Summary
step_2.details: Document all events chronologically with dates, participants, and supporting evidence

step_3.title: Draft Motion to Vacate (Inquest)
step_3.details: File motion based on excusable default due to hospitalization
documents_needed: Timeline of hospitalization vs. inquest date

step_4.title: Draft Motion to Vacate (Eviction)
step_4.details: File motion challenging wrongful eviction and stay violation
documents_needed: Copy of OSC and stay order

step_5.title: File Poor Person Affidavit (In Forma Pauperis)
step_5.details: Request court fee waivers and improve eligibility for legal aid

step_6.title: Notify Legal Aid Society
step_6.details: Submit proof of open/active case to Legal Aid for representation

step_7.title: Prepare for Court Hearing
step_7.details: Organize evidence, medical records, affidavits for court appearance
step_7.accommodations: Request accessible court date given disability/medical needs

step_8.title: Coordinate Civil Suits
step_8.details: Ensure all 4 civil lawsuits are documented and progressing

step_9.title: Work with Assigned Counsel
step_9.details: Once Legal Aid assigns attorney, provide all documents and timeline

key_documents_needed: Original court order establishing lifetime tenancy
Copy of OSC (Order to Show Cause) with stay
Storage facility receipt and property list
Hospital discharge summaries for relevant dates
Timeline of missed court dates vs. hospitalizations
Police report (if available for train incident)
notes: This case involves complex intersection of housing law, wrongful eviction, property loss, and disability rights. Strong grounds exist for relief on multiple fronts. Legal Aid Society support pending active case status. Medical documentation is critical to all relief motions.
